ActionItems: Confirm October Tangerine editorial day rate ($2,000) and lock Oatly November content brief with Tomás before quarter-end.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Break-even analysis for Jesse Page Photography 2026, evaluating the rate-band mix and retainer thresholds needed to hit the $110K annual revenue target.

CurrentState.Note: Baseline built from 2025 YTD Stripe deposits, Oatly monthly retainer, and Tangerine quarterly editorial cadence; assumes stable dairy-free catering costs.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Current $92K gross covers overhead and $22K quarterly taxes with a $1,500/month savings buffer; hitting $110K requires two additional editorial day-rate shoots or an added brand retainer.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Eight restaurant menu half-days per year at $1,200-$1,500 each cover fixed overhead (Adobe, Squarespace, insurance) plus one quarterly tax payment.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Preserves buffer for lean months and dairy-free scouting cost; no rate increase required to hit baseline.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Seasonal menu reshoot

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Editorial day rates ($1,800-$2,200) plus adjacent brand deliverables cover prep and travel overhead; adds cushion for quarterly tax quarters but risks pushing smaller brand clients toward one-off deliverables.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Editorial shoot prep
